terms.definition: A dict subclass from collections that counts hashable items and supports most_common for ranking by frequency.
A dict subclass that calls a factory to supply a default value for missing keys, simplifying grouping and accumulation.
A double-ended queue with O(1) appends/pops at both ends and an optional maxlen for fixed-size sliding windows.
A factory for tuple subclasses with named, immutable fields — a lightweight record type.
A decorator that auto-generates __init__, __repr__ and __eq__ for classes that mainly store data.
A dataclass field option that supplies a fresh mutable default (e.g. list) per instance, avoiding shared-state bugs.
A dataclass created with frozen=True, making instances immutable and hashable.
